**Result**
- 0 P0 / 0 P1 / 0 P2 / 1 P3 (non-blocking, defense-in-depth)
- `no_unresolved_p0: true`, `no_unresolved_p1: true`

**What I verified directly (read-only):** The CP676 binding, descriptor functions, validators, tests, and both contract files are mutually consistent and match the pack baseline exactly (150 units, correct range, exact deliverable/micro-title distributions). The pack is descriptor-only with every runtime/write flag closed, a complete no-write/no-real-data attestation, Claude held as read-only non-final reviewer, and Hermes confined to deterministic gate handling — not product runtime. The pack correctly sits in its pre-review state (`production_ready: false`, `pending_review`), and review-hardening preconditions (auth-preflight valid JSON, read-tools-only, summary-only prohibited for risk C) all hold.

**Provenance caveat:** command-evidence.json's 22/22 passing validation commands are self-reported by the build process; I could not re-execute them under read-only constraints, but static inspection of the validators/tests/contracts they exercise corroborates the reported outcomes.

**The single P3:** the standalone per-key no-write enforcement in `validators.js:1463-1469` re-asserts only `cp666_no_write_attestation`; the current pack's `cp676_no_write_attestation` relies on the shared frozen constant plus projection deep-equality. Directly verified: (1) registry.js:1633-1881 CP676 binding+requirements match the pack baseline exactly — unit_count 150, range, deliverable distribution {test:13,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:11,implementation:53,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3} and micro-title distribution both sum to 150; (2) service.js:2204-2406 descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff functions are descriptor-only with every runtime flag false, Claude read-only and not promoted to final approval, handoff to CP00-677/RP22.P04.M03.S21; (3) validators.js:583-612 and 1273-1380 enforce descriptor_only/no-runtime/no-raw-payload/customer-safe and Claude-not-final, chained into the contract-projection validation; (4) model.test.js:286-296,519-563 assert coverage valid and boundaries closed; (5) both contract files are generated_from_pack_id CP00-676 with a complete no-write/no-real-data attestation (all write/runtime keys false, claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false); (6) index.js re-exports all CP676 symbols; (7) plan live_state and next-pack-queue agree on CP00-676 → RP22.P04.M03.S21. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review-packet/evidence-gate handling (H22), not product runtime (runtime_receipt_emitted=false). Claude remains read-only and is not the final approver; the enterprise-trust/human-approval boundary is closed outside this receipt, consistent with the authority boundary. Review-hardening checks pass: readiness auth-preflight succeeded with well-formed JSON (no auth failure, non-zero stdout), read-tools-only mode, no forbidden tools, summary-only correctly prohibited for a risk-C pack with security/review deliverables. Provenance note: the 22/22 validation commands in command-evidence.json (npm test, full validate suite, build, git diff --check) are self-reported by the construction process; as a read-only reviewer I did not re-execute them, but static inspection of the validators, tests, and contracts they exercise corroborates the reported PASS outcomes.
